campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed extending a temporary rule (ordinance 2326) and moved forward on plans for a bicycle bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including applying for grant funding to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 youth scholarship donation from Water Tower Kitchen
Extending temporary ordinance 2326
Bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application for Hamilton Avenue overcross project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing to get community input on a proposed new rule or regulation. After listening to residents' comments, the council decided to move forward with introducing the ordinance and skipped the usual first reading step to speed up the process. They directed staff to add it to the next meeting's agenda so the council can vote on final approval.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident feedback
First reading of ordinance waived to expedite approval process
Ordinance scheduled for adoption vote at next regular meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda summary

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: Los Altos City Council met to approve several routine matters including a report on their 2026 weed removal efforts and new rules for how the council operates. The council also approved raising two flags at city hall—one for Pride Month and one for Juneteenth—and discussed housing affordability policies and transportation authority updates.
keyTopics: Weed abatement completion report for 2026
New City Council operating procedures and norms
Affordable housing unit transfer rules
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag raising approvals
Housing element implementation and VTA transportation updates

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met to review how the city is doing financially in early 2026 and approve several planning projects. They also discussed progress on the city's housing and general development plans, approved a new residential tract development called Solana Project, and set priorities for community health and senior services.
keyTopics: January 2026 financial and investment report
Solana Project residential development (Tract 10657) approval
Housing Element and General Plan progress update
2026 Community Health and Senior Services priorities
Changes to how council meetings are documented

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting on April 1, 2026 to prepare for two upcoming council meetings scheduled for April 7 and April 14. They reviewed and organized the agendas for both meetings, deciding which items to add, which topics needed specific time slots, and handling any required legal notice requirements.
keyTopics: Finalized April 7, 2026 council meeting agenda
Organized April 14, 2026 draft agenda for upcoming decisions
Set time limits for specific agenda items when necessary
Reviewed legal notification requirements for agenda items
Reviewed public records from March 19-26, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a routine meeting on March 30, 2026, where they approved previous meeting minutes and opened the floor for public comments. The council reviewed committee reports and set schedules for upcoming board and commission meetings throughout the city.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period
Committee and staff reports
Upcoming board and commission meeting schedules
Documents available at City Clerk's Office (500 Castro Street)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 6, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Library Board of Trustees held a meeting on April 6, 2026, to celebrate National Library Week and discuss what the library is doing for young kids in the community. The board also gave an update on how the library's different branch locations are doing.
keyTopics: National Library Week celebration
Early learning programs and initiatives for children
Updates on library branch operations
Library Trustees Board meeting

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses thrive by launching several new programs. They discussed a license amnesty program to help businesses get compliant, plans to boost shopping centers, and a summer campaign to encourage residents to shop locally. The council also explored ways to partner with private companies to sponsor city events and offer financial incentives for retail stores, small businesses, and startups.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program for non-compliant local businesses
Summer 'Shop Local' campaign to boost Cupertino retail
Partnerships with private companies to sponsor city events
Financial incentives for retail, small businesses, and startups
Outreach efforts to shopping centers and business owners

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara held a Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing on April 2, 2026, where the city reviewed historical sites and landmark designations. Residents could attend in person or join remotely via Zoom to participate in the discussion and provide feedback.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment submission deadline (12 p.m. meeting day)
Remote participation via Zoom or phone call-in option

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine meeting on March 17, 2026 to handle administrative items and approve several appointments and travel requests. The council reviewed meeting schedules, approved previous meeting minutes, and authorized Mayor Montano to attend the WELL 2026 Annual conference.
keyTopics: Mayor Montano approved to travel to WELL 2026 Annual conference
New School District representative appointed to Economic Development position
City Council meeting calendar and schedule confirmed
Previous meeting minutes approved
Upcoming agenda items previewed for next meeting

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026, to handle several routine matters including recognizing Women's History Month. The council approved an agreement with the American Red Cross to use city facilities during emergencies, and authorized a three-year contract with Insight Public Sector to provide email security and backup services for about $93,400 per year.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
American Red Cross disaster facility agreement
Email security software contract ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda items added to meeting
